item.title: David Armstrong’s Probing Gaze
item.description: Also: Jennifer Tilly in the surreal world of “The Adding Machine,” New York City Ballet’s spring season, Anne Hathaway and Michaela Coel in “Mother Mary,” and more.

item.title: A Reading List from the Director of the Noguchi Museum
item.description: Amy Hau discusses some books by Marilyn Chase, Karin Higa, and Edmund de Waal that have guided her work as a curator.

item.title: J. D. Vance’s Bumpy Ride
item.description: It wasn’t the first time that Trump had debased someone who serves him. It wasn’t even the first time that Vance had had to downplay a blasphemy-themed A.I. image.
item.dc:creator: ByAmy Davidson Sorkin

item.title: The Popes That Trump Might’ve Liked
item.description: The President thinks Pope Leo XIV is a wuss. Meet some real tough-guy Pontiffs who might have fit the bill.
item.dc:creator: ByJane Bua

item.title: When Soul Food Met Daniel Boulud
item.description: The Harlem franchise Charles Pan-Fried Chicken invited a bunch of chefs to take over for the weekend. Up next: oxtails from Lana Lagomarsini.
item.dc:creator: ByZach Helfand

item.title: Daphne Rubin-Vega Comes Home
item.description: Strolling through Hell’s Kitchen, the actress recalls old celeb sightings (Jane Fonda! Donald Sutherland!) on her way to playing the swaggering Mr. Zero in “The Adding Machine,” Off Broadway.
item.dc:creator: BySarah Larson

item.title: The Anatomy of a Failure
item.description: From spray-on condoms to radioactive wrinkle cream, “Flops?!,” at the Musée des Arts et Métiers, in Paris, puts terrible inventions in the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: ByLauren Collins

item.title: When Your Digital Life Vanishes
item.description: A broken phone or corrupted drive can mean the loss of work, evidence, art, or the last traces of the dead. But sometimes data-recovery experts can summon lost files from the void.
item.dc:creator: ByJulian Lucas

item.title: Tabula Rasa: Volume Six
item.description: A project meant not to end.
item.dc:creator: ByJohn McPhee

item.title: How Professional Wrestling Prepared Linda McMahon for Trump’s Cabinet
item.description: The Education Secretary ran the W.W.E. for years with her husband, Vince, an unstable man who, like her new boss, has a genius for inflaming the crowd.
item.dc:creator: ByZach Helfand

item.title: The Action-Film Director Who’s Taking On Michael Jackson
item.description: Antoine Fuqua has built a career on movies with irresistible heroes. Now he’s telling the story of the King of Pop.
item.dc:creator: ByKelefa Sanneh

item.title: Escape Rooms for Middle-Aged People
item.description: Work as a team as you and other dads chat about pro sports, college sports, kids (and their sports), while avoiding eye contact, politics, and any hint of vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: ByCharles Yu

item.title: “Ordinary Wear and Tear”
item.description: She broke Carl’s heart, he thought, but she’s not breaking mine.
item.dc:creator: ByThomas McGuane

item.title: Was Raphael the Runt of the Renaissance?
item.description: Many have called him boring, a peddler of simpleminded beauty. At the Met, a blockbuster exhibition restores his standing.
item.dc:creator: ByZachary Fine

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: “The Power of Life,” “How It Feels to Be Alive,” “Go Gentle,” and “Exemplary Humans.”

item.title: In Defense of the Moderate
item.description: In an era that prizes passion, “reasonableness” gets caricatured as political cowardice or bloodless neutrality. A new book says it’s exactly what we need.
item.dc:creator: ByNikhil Krishnan

item.title: The Novelist Reimagining the Japanese American Internment
item.description: In “Questions 27 & 28,” Karen Tei Yamashita opens an inquiry into how the story of the past gets made.
item.dc:creator: ByHua Hsu

item.title: Sharp Claws at “Becky Shaw” and “Cats: The Jellicle Ball”
item.description: Gina Gionfriddo’s zinger-filled sex farce and the celebratory ballroom-culture adaptation of Andrew Lloyd Webber’s confounding musical are cathartic catnip.
item.dc:creator: ByEmily Nussbaum

item.title: The Calculated Uplift of “I Swear”
item.description: Kirk Jones’s bio-pic of the activist John Davidson, who has worked to destigmatize Tourette’s syndrome, is effective as an educational tool but mechanical as a drama.
item.dc:creator: ByJustin Chang

item.title: “Spring Comes and I Finally Throw Out the Last Flowers I Bought You”
item.description: “It’s been weeks. / It’s been months. It’s been seasons.”
item.dc:creator: ByAriel Francisco

item.title: “Favor”
item.description: “My death is in the second drawer.”
item.dc:creator: ByFranz Wright

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the April 27, 2026, magazine.

item.title: The Crossword: Wednesday, April 15, 2026
item.description: A beginner-friendly puzzle.
item.dc:creator: ByRobyn Weintraub
